title: Projects
text:
Research Projects

Working Paper

Pre-move Publication-Location Exposure and Destination Choice among Elite Biomedical Scientists
Status. Working Paper
Research question. How is a scientist’s pre-move publication-location exposure associated with observed destination choice among candidate cities?
Data. Event-specific destination-choice data derived from elite biomedical scientist mobility records.
Methods. Event-specific candidate sets, origin-anchored pre-move exposure measures, conditional logit models, candidate-set checks, and coverage diagnostics.
Output. Working paper.



Accepted Article

Spatial Structure and Determinants of Top Global Biomedical Scientists’ Mobility from an Urban Network Perspective
Status. Accepted Article
Research question. How can the mobility of top global biomedical scientists be described from an urban network perspective?
Data. Scientist mobility and city-level network data assembled for the article.
Methods. Urban network analysis and empirical analysis of spatial structure and related factors.
Output. Accepted article.



Master’s Thesis

Intercity Mobility Network of Top Global Biomedical Scientists: A Dual Perspective of Whole and Ego Networks
Status. Master’s Thesis
Research question. What patterns appear in the intercity mobility network of top global biomedical scientists from whole-network and ego-network perspectives?
Data. Mobility records and city-level network data assembled for the master’s thesis.
Methods. Whole-network analysis, ego-network analysis, and spatial interpretation of mobility patterns.
Output. Master’s thesis completed at East China Normal University.



Ongoing Research

Global Mobility and Collaboration Networks of Elite Biomedical Scientists
Status. Ongoing Research
Research question. How are mobility, collaboration, institutions, and cities connected in longitudinal scientific career data?
Data. Longitudinal scientist-institution-city data under construction.
Methods. Directed weighted mobility networks, ego-network indicators, collaboration networks, and WERGM-based specifications.
Output. Ongoing research extension of earlier thesis and article work.



Public Repository

Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit
Status. Public Repository
Research question. How can author-institution-city linkage and geocoding workflows be audited before bibliographic data are used for research on scientific geography or mobility?
Data. Fully synthetic demonstration records.
Methods. Schema validation, identifier linkage, institution normalization, city-country validation, QA checks, duplicate diagnostics, confidence diagnostics, and coverage reporting.
Output. Public GitHub repository.

title: Ran Wang
text:
中文

Ran Wang


Urban & Regional Economics · Scientific Mobility · Regional Innovation


I am an empirical researcher trained in economic geography. I study scientific mobility, institutional platforms, and regional innovation using longitudinal data on scientists, institutions, publications, and cities. My work combines discrete-choice models, network analysis, and spatial data methods.


View Research Download CV Selected Projects GitHub


Photo: John Rodenn Castillo / Unsplash



Research Focus


My research examines scientific mobility, institutional platforms, and regional innovation through longitudinal data on scientists, institutions, publications, and cities. I focus on clear definitions of affiliation, mobility, collaboration, and place, and on empirical designs that connect individual trajectories with urban and regional research systems.




Research Themes


Scientific Mobility and Destination Choice. I study how scientists move across institutions and cities, with attention to information available before a move. Current work compares observed destinations with event-specific candidate cities.


Regional Innovation and Urban Networks. This work examines how cities and regions differ in research capacity, institutional platforms, and positions within mobility and collaboration networks. The emphasis is on observed associations rather than causal claims beyond the research design.


Relational Data and Network Methods. I build scientist-institution-city data that keep affiliations, publications, collaboration ties, and mobility events analytically distinct. This supports network analysis, spatial comparison, and reproducible workflow checks.




Selected Work


Accepted Article. Spatial Structure and Determinants of Top Global Biomedical Scientists’ Mobility from an Urban Network Perspective.


Working Paper. Pre-move Publication-Location Exposure and Destination Choice among Elite Biomedical Scientists.


Public Repository. Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit. View repository




Quick Links


Research Projects CV Contact

title: Research
text:
中文


Introduction


My research sits at the intersection of economic geography, urban and regional economics, and science of science. I study how scientific mobility, institutional affiliations, collaboration relations, and city-level research systems are associated with the spatial organization of knowledge production.


The common thread is empirical work that treats scientists, institutions, publications, and cities as linked but distinct units of analysis. This helps separate observed affiliations, employment histories, collaboration ties, and mobility events before applying statistical or network models.




Research Themes


Scientific Mobility and Career Trajectories. I examine how elite biomedical scientists move across institutions and cities over time. Current work links career histories with publication-location exposure and destination choices while keeping the observational design explicit.


Regional Innovation and Urban Networks. I study how cities and regions differ in research capacity, institutional diversity, and positions within mobility and collaboration networks. This work connects urban research systems with observed patterns of high-skilled mobility and knowledge production.


Science of Science and Relational Data. I build and audit datasets that connect scientists, institutions, publications, and places. The goal is to make relational data usable for research on scientific geography while keeping linkage, coverage, and geocoding uncertainty visible.




Data and Methods



Longitudinal scientist-institution-city data


Discrete-choice models


Network analysis


Spatial data methods


Reproducible workflows in Python and R





Current Research Agenda


Destination choice among elite biomedical scientists. This project studies whether a scientist’s pre-move publication-location exposure is related to the destination city selected from an event-specific candidate set.


Mobility and collaboration networks. This line of work extends earlier thesis and article research on intercity mobility networks by developing longitudinal scientist-institution-city data and directed weighted network indicators.


Public workflow audit for author-institution-city linkage. The public repository uses synthetic records to demonstrate schema validation, institution normalization, geocoding checks, and documentation patterns for reproducible research workflows.

title: CV
text:
中文


I am trained in human geography, with research interests in scientific mobility, regional innovation, urban networks, and empirical work using longitudinal relational data. This page is a short public overview; the PDF contains the full public CV.


Curriculum Vitae


Download CV




Education


East China Normal University. M.Sc. in Human Geography, 2023-2026. School of Geographic Sciences; economic geography and regional innovation.


Northeast Normal University. B.Sc. in Human Geography and Urban-Rural Planning, 2019-2023. Training in quantitative geography, GIS applications, urban geography, regional analysis, and planning.




Research Outputs



Spatial Structure and Determinants of Top Global Biomedical Scientists’ Mobility from an Urban Network Perspective — accepted article.


Intercity Mobility Network of Top Global Biomedical Scientists: A Dual Perspective of Whole and Ego Networks — master’s thesis.


Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit — public code repository.





Methods and Skills


Python; R; Stata; GIS; social network analysis; ego-network analysis; conditional logit models; ERGM/WERGM; entity resolution; longitudinal multi-source data construction; coverage audits.
